name: Green Chili with Pork
recipeIngredient: 2 4-oz cans sliced jalapenos or chopped green chiles
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium to large pot with a fitted lid, heat the peanut oil over medium heat.
2. Add the chopped garlic cloves and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
3. Remove the garlic cloves with a slotted spoon and set aside.
4. Trim excess fat from the pork and cut into ¾-inch cubes.
5. In a large container with a tight-fitting lid, mix together the flour, garlic salt, ground pepper, and cumin.
6. Add the cubed pork to the mixture and stir until well coated.
7. Carefully add the pork to the hot oil in batches, shaking the container to prevent burning.
8. Brown the pork, stirring constantly and scraping the bottom of the pot as needed.
9. Once the pork is browned, remove it from the pan with a slotted spoon.
10. In the same pan, sauté the sliced jalapenos or chopped green chiles until lightly softened.
11. Return the pork to the pan with the vegetables.
12. Dissolve the beef bouillon cube and chicken bouillon cube in about ¼ cup of hot water.
13. Add this broth to the pot and stir well to combine.
14. Gradually add the remaining flour and spice mixture, stirring continuously until a medium-thick sauce forms.
15. Add enough water to reach a desired consistency, then simmer on low heat for 3 hours, stirring occasionally.
16. Adjust seasoning as needed before serving.
This dish can be served over a beef or bean burrito with shredded lettuce and tomato, sour cream, and avocado, or used as a filling for a corn tortilla.
